title: Complex Numbers
description: The imaginary unit, complex arithmetic, the Argand diagram, conjugates and division, modulus-argument (polar) form, and de Moivre's theorem.
objectives: Explain why the imaginary unit i is introduced, and write complex numbers in the standard form a + bi
Add, subtract, and multiply complex numbers, simplifying using i^2 = -1
Represent a complex number as a point on the Argand diagram
Find the complex conjugate of a complex number and use it to divide complex numbers
Calculate the modulus and argument of a complex number and convert between Cartesian and modulus-argument (polar) form
Multiply and divide complex numbers in modulus-argument form, and apply de Moivre's theorem to find integer powers of a complex number

lessons.title: Imaginary numbers, complex arithmetic, and the Argand diagram
Multiplying, dividing, and de Moivre's theorem
